response.justification: Strong software development background with C/C++ proficiency and systems-level experience, but lacks the explicitly required native-level Japanese language proficiency—a non-negotiable requirement. Additionally, the candidate lacks demonstrated expertise in security technologies (encryption, authentication, attack detection), networking protocols (L2/L3, NETCONF, SNMP), and embedded security product development. Recent career focus on web/cloud platforms (TypeScript, AWS) rather than embedded networking or security firmware limits domain alignment.
strengths: Mid-Senior level with 15+ years cross-domain software development including C/C++ systems programming and low-latency infrastructure work
Proven Linux kernel and systems-level experience, with demonstrated ability to work with hardware control and embedded platforms
Strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD pipelines, and quality-focused development methodology
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency demonstrated; role explicitly requires native-level competency as mandatory qualification
No security domain expertise visible: missing encryption/key exchange, authentication systems, attack detection, or tamper detection experience
Career trajectory over last 5 years focused on web/cloud platforms; no embedded networking, LAN-switch products, or security firmware product experience
